page: Microsoft Azure ND40rs_v2 is an NDSv2-family virtual machine designed for high-performance computing and accelerated workloads. Powered by an Intel processor on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 40 dedicated vCPUs managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The hardware configuration includes 672.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 16.8 GB of RAM per core.

For accelerated computing, the ND40rs_v2 is equipped with eight NVIDIA V100 GPUs, delivering a combined total of 238 GB of GPU VRAM. The server also features 2970 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. It is RDMA-capable, enabling low-latency, high-throughput networking across clustered nodes.

Given its hardware profile, this server offers a high-density resource configuration suitable for large-scale parallel processing. The combination of multiple GPUs, substantial system memory, and RDMA capabilities makes this instance type a strong candidate for distributed machine learning training, deep learning, high-performance computing simulations, and large-scale database workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure ND40rs_v2 is an NDSv2-family virtual machine optimized for GPU-accelerated workloads and high-performance computing. Built on an Intel x86_64 architecture and managed by Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 40 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding 16.8 GB of RAM per core. The server is equipped with eight NVIDIA V100 GPUs providing a total of 238 GB of VRAM, alongside 2970 GB of local storage. With support for Premium Storage and RDMA networking, this instance is designed for clustered, high-throughput environments. The hardware profile makes it highly suitable for deep learning training, complex scientific simulations, and memory-intensive database operations.

tagline: An accelerated virtual machine featuring eight dedicated GPUs, massive system memory, and low-latency RDMA networking for high-performance computing workloads.
